not just, uh, their discretion,

that most New Zealand schools would see as legitimate.
Okay. I'm gonna go to Dr. Nan.
Thank you Chair. Um, just a question to you.

to democracy

and it's a move towards fascism.
Yeah, I mean, as, as I said earlier, um, if,
if people are worried or are concerned about potentially

you know, far fewer people
will, will feel comfortable doing.
I mean, I, I myself wouldn't, you know, I I,
my job depends on me not having a criminal record.
Um, so it, you know, it would make it a lot harder for me
to go and exercise my political rights to, uh, you know, to,
to peaceful protests.
Alright. Um, uh, Ms.
Cle, but just this is the last question.
Um, Tom, the,
the new offense we are hoping to, um, to
make into law is specifically this engaging in targeted
and disruptive demonstration near residential premises.
It's not protesting that we are disallow protests.
It's not saying you can't protest altogether, it's engaging
in targeted and disruptive
demonstration near residential premises.

that with the people that are concerned

This is a targeted new offense that we're bringing in
to make sure that people feel safe in their homes.
Um, certainly,
but I think miss you need to reflect on what,
what a protest actually is.

That's not a protest, right?
I'm not talking about people hurling abuse either.
Um, what I'm talking about is people making themselves,
making their demands aware.

You're certainly, yeah. Yeah.
And you know what,
what police officer is not gonna hear loud noise in the
